world_politics.titulo: Spotlight on the continued decline of press freedom globally
world_politics.resumo: Press freedom has fallen to its lowest level in 25 years. world_politics.titulo: Germany news: Majority thinks coalition government doomed — survey
world_politics.resumo: More than half of people in Germany believe the coalition government will collapse before its term is up. The head of a leading economic think tank says US tariffs could trigger a German recession. DW has more.

world_politics.titulo: Huge crowd attends free Shakira Copacabana beach concert

world_politics.resumo: The Colombian superstar delivers one of the biggest shows of her career, as fans flock from Brazil and beyond for her "Women No Longer Cry" world tour.

world_politics.titulo: Trump says cutting US troops in Germany 'a lot further'
world_politics.resumo: Germany's defense minister has said Europe needs to take more responsibility for its own security. Top Republicans warn reducing troop numbers sends the "wrong signal" to Russian President Vladimir Putin.

world_politics.titulo: A Perilous Train Ride Home in Pakistan on the Jaffer Express
world_politics.resumo: We witnessed crossfire while waiting for the Jaffer Express, a vital lifeline through Balochistan. For many, it’s the only way home.

world_politics.titulo: How A.I. Is Transforming China’s Entertainment Industry

world_politics.titulo: An Insurgency Threatens U.S. Mining Ambitions in Pakistan
world_politics.resumo: Attacks by the Baloch Liberation Army could derail Pakistan’s plans for a billion-dollar mining deal with the Trump administration.

world_politics.titulo: Alex Zanardi, F1 driver and multiple CART and Paralympic champion, dies at 59
world_politics.resumo: Italian race driver turned Paralympian Alex Zanardi has died aged 59. The former Formula 1 driver and two-time CART champion lost his legs in a horrific crash at the peak of his powers, but his drive to compete endured.

world_politics.titulo: Jordanians Struggle as Mideast Wars Scare Tourists Away
world_politics.resumo: Though Jordan mostly sat out the conflicts, its tourism high season was nearly wiped out at popular sites. Visitors canceled flights, hotels and tours.

world_politics.resumo: U.S. officials say they will “unleash prosperity” by commandeering the oil industry. Many people in Caracas say it will take far more than that.

world_politics.resumo: Actors and writers created by artificial intelligence may be able to write and appear in films, but they will not qualify for Oscars under new rules issued by the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences, which address the use of AI for the first time. world_politics.titulo: Iran: Jailed Nobel winner Narges Mohammadi hospitalized
world_politics.resumo: Iranian activist Narges Mohammadi is in hospital after suffering a "cardiac crisis" in prison, her family says. Mohammadi won the 2023 Nobel Peace Prize for her advocacy of women's rights and freedom.

world_politics.resumo: In the Gazan city of Khan Younis, workers are still clearing rubble seven months after the ceasefire that followed two years of war. The work continues around the clock, but it brings in only a few euros a day, barely enough to support their families. Liza Kaminov has more.

world_politics.titulo: How the Iran war is hurting travelers, airline industry

world_politics.resumo: The airline had been in talks with the Trump administration about a $500m bailout.

world_politics.resumo: Cherry blossom viewing, or hanami, is a cherished rite in Japan. Some Japanese are also doing it in South Korea.

world_politics.resumo: The average wait for a test in England and Scotland has hit 22 weeks, up from five weeks before the pandemic, leading to frustration and attempts to jump the line.
